title: Document MLS state machine — implicit transitions tangled in handle_relay_event
description:
MLS group lifecycle transitions are split across two relay message handlers with implicit rules:

DirectoryList (lines 177-227):
    if i_am_admin && !state.mls_active {
        create_group() → mls_active = true → publish room key

WhisperDeliver (lines 434-478):
    if !state.mls_active {
        join_from_welcome() → mls_active = true → publish room key

Reconnect (lines 100-118):
    mls_active = false, reset MLS client

Implicit state machine:
    Inactive → (admin creates) → Active
    Inactive → (peer receives Welcome) → Active
    Any → (reconnect) → Inactive

Problems:
- No single place documents valid transitions
- Hard to reason about when room_hpke_secret is valid
- Adding new transitions (e.g., group updates) risks inconsistency
- Testing MLS logic requires triggering specific relay events

Solution: Create explicit MLS state abstraction with invariant checks:
    impl AppState {
        async fn activate_mls_as_admin(...) -> Result<()>
        async fn activate_mls_from_welcome(...) -> Result<()>
        fn deactivate_mls_on_reconnect()
    }

This will:
- Document the state machine explicitly
- Prevent invalid transitions
- Make testing easier
close_reason: deferred to backlog; P3/P4 style issues, not blocking epic completion
